quiz.question:
Black is two pawns up. 

But one of the pawns is backwards on h6, and the other pawn (c3) is blockaded by White. 

Is this winning for Black?

answers.text: Yes; Black can create a passed pawn easily. h5 is a threat to do this and this is completely winning for Black.

feedback.text:
Exactly! 

White has some weaknesses and the bishop cannot defend both of them.

distractors.text: This is a draw because the pawn on c3 is sufficiently blockaded. Black doesn't have any practical chances.

feedback.text: Are you sure? White has many weaknesses.

distractors.text: This is objectively a draw, but Black has practical winning chances by trying to create complications.

feedback.text: Are you sure the chances are only practical?
